subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — Go Math Texas: Beginning-of-Year Test

standards.subject: math
standards.description: Represent fractions with models / equal parts

standards.subject: math
standards.description: Tell time to the nearest minute — prerequisite

prompt: Evan has 54 pencils. He places the same number of pencils in each of 6 pencil cases. Which related fact shows the number of pencils in each pencil case?

options.rationale: 18 pencils per case would not split 54 into 6 equal groups.
This is subtraction, not the related multiplication/division fact.
Subtraction does not show equal groups of 6.

explanation: 54 ÷ 6 = 9, and the related multiplication fact is 9 × 6 = 54, so each case holds 9 pencils.

prompt: Mari is drawing a sketch for a flower garden. Each unit square is 1 square foot. Which multiplication equation can be used to find the area of the flower garden?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 172 120" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="8" y="8" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="34" y="8" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="60" y="8" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="86" y="8" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="112" y="8" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="138" y="8" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="8" y="34" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="34" y="34" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="60" y="34" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="86" y="34" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="112" y="34" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="138" y="34" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="8" y="60" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="34" y="60" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="60" y="60" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="86" y="60" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="112" y="60" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="138" y="60" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="8" y="86" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="34" y="86" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="60" y="86" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="86" y="86" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="112" y="86" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="138" y="86" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>

options.rationale: Area is found by multiplying, not adding, the side lengths.
The grid is 4 by 6, not 4 by 5.

explanation: The garden is 4 rows by 6 columns of unit squares, so area = 4 × 6 = 24 square feet.

prompt: Carmen is putting tape around the edge of a poster. The poster has side lengths of 3 feet and 4 feet. How many feet of tape will Carmen need?

options.rationale: 7 is just 3 + 4 (one length and one width); a rectangle has two of each.
12 is the area (3 × 4), not the distance around.

explanation: Perimeter of a rectangle = 2 × (length + width) = 2 × (3 + 4) = 14 feet.

prompt: Mr. Bonta ate the part of a pie that is shaded. What fraction of the pie did he eat?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 120 120" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M60,60 L60.0,10.0 A50,50 0 0 1 103.3,35.0 Z" fill="#cfcfcf" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L103.3,35.0 A50,50 0 0 1 103.3,85.0 Z" fill="#cfcfcf" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L103.3,85.0 A50,50 0 0 1 60.0,110.0 Z" fill="#cfcfcf" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L60.0,110.0 A50,50 0 0 1 16.7,85.0 Z" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L16.7,85.0 A50,50 0 0 1 16.7,35.0 Z" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L16.7,35.0 A50,50 0 0 1 60.0,10.0 Z" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>
figures.alt: pie in 6 equal parts with 3 shaded

options.rationale: More than one part is shaded.
Fewer than five parts are shaded.

explanation: The pie has 6 equal parts and 3 are shaded, so 3/6 of the pie was eaten. [Figure shading is faint in the original scan; 3/6 (half the pie) is the most defensible reading.]

prompt: Devin earns $8 per hour at a bakery. How much does he earn in 9 hours?

options.rationale: That is 9 × 9; he earns $8 per hour.

explanation: Equal pay each hour: 8 × 9 = 72 dollars.

prompt: The number of people attending a baseball game is 1,402. Which base-ten model shows the number 1,402?

options.rationale: 1,402 has 0 tens; this shows a ten.
Wrong number of hundreds and tens.

explanation: 1,402 = 1 thousand + 4 hundreds + 0 tens + 2 ones, so the model has 1 large cube, 4 flats, no rods, and 2 small cubes. [The original shows block pictures; the correct one has no tens rods.]

prompt: Which set of numbers contains all odd numbers?
explanation: A number is odd if its ones digit is 1, 3, 5, 7, or 9. Only 503, 217, 851 are all odd.

prompt: Which fraction names the point closest to 0 on the number line?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 380 80" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="18" y1="40" x2="362" y2="40" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#a)" marker-start="url(#b)"/><defs><marker id="a" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6" fill="black"/></marker><marker id="b" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="0" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M6,0 L0,3 L6,6" fill="black"/></marker></defs><line x1="30.0" y1="33" x2="30.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="30.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">0</text><line x1="110.0" y1="33" x2="110.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="110.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle"></text><line x1="190.0" y1="33" x2="190.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="190.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle"></text><line x1="270.0" y1="33" x2="270.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="270.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle"></text><line x1="350.0" y1="33" x2="350.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="350.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1</text></svg>
figures.alt: number line 0 to 1 divided into 4 equal parts

explanation: The line from 0 to 1 is split into 4 equal parts. The mark closest to 0 is one part from 0, which is 1/4.

prompt: There are 2 barns on a horse ranch. Each barn has 4 rows of horse stalls. There are 20 stalls in each row. Which equation shows the Commutative Property of multiplication?

options.rationale: Both sides are identical; no property is shown.

explanation: The Commutative Property changes the order of factors: 2 × 4 × 20 = 2 × 20 × 4.

prompt: Carter starts practicing piano at 3:45 P.M. He finishes 30 minutes later. At what time does he finish?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 140 140" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><circle cx="70" cy="70" r="58" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="3"/><text x="92.0" y="36.9" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1</text><text x="108.1" y="53.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">2</text><text x="114.0" y="75.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">3</text><text x="108.1" y="97.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">4</text><text x="92.0" y="113.1" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">5</text><text x="70.0" y="119.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">6</text><text x="48.0" y="113.1" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">7</text><text x="31.9" y="97.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">8</text><text x="26.0" y="75.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">9</text><text x="31.9" y="53.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">10</text><text x="48.0" y="36.9" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">11</text><text x="70.0" y="31.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">12</text><line x1="70" y1="70" x2="30.0" y2="70.0" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="70" y1="70" x2="95.9" y2="80.7" stroke="black" stroke-width="3"/><circle cx="70" cy="70" r="3" fill="black"/></svg>

options.rationale: That goes back 30 minutes instead of forward.
That adds a whole hour, not 30 minutes.

explanation: 30 minutes after 3:45 is 4:15 (15 minutes to 4:00, then 15 more).

prompt: Jack finished 3/8 of his math problems. Which shows 3/8 written as a sum of unit fractions?

options.rationale: That equals 9/8, and 3/8 is not a unit fraction.
These have different denominators and do not total 3/8.

explanation: A unit fraction has 1 on top. 3/8 = 1/8 + 1/8 + 1/8.

prompt: Deena gets a $20 allowance every month. She saves $7 of her allowance each month. How much will she save in 5 months?

options.rationale: That is not 7 saved each of 5 months.
That is 20 × 5, her whole allowance, not her savings.

explanation: She saves the same amount each month: 7 × 5 = 35 dollars.

prompt: Katherine wants to buy 2 books. Each book costs $14. She has $20 saved and will borrow the rest from her sister. She will pay her sister back $4 each week. How many weeks will it take to pay back her sister?
explanation: Two books cost 2 × 14 = $28. She borrows 28 − 20 = $8. At $4 per week, 8 ÷ 4 = 2 weeks.

prompt: Drew has 53 trading cards. He gives 18 to his brother. How many trading cards does he have left?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 42 42" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="8" y="8" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>
figures.alt: strip diagram: 53 total, 18 given away, ? left

explanation: Subtract the cards given away: 53 − 18 = 35.

prompt: Brett stacked 15 boxes of markers. He places his stacks in 3 rows with the same number of boxes in each row. How many boxes are in each row?

options.rationale: That is the number of rows, not boxes per row.

explanation: Share equally into 3 rows: 15 ÷ 3 = 5 boxes per row.

prompt: Brooke folds her socks in sets of 2. She folds 18 socks. How many sets of socks does she fold?
explanation: Each set has 2 socks: 18 ÷ 2 = 9 sets.

prompt: Ricardo has two $10 bills, four $1 bills, two quarters, and one dime. How much money does Ricardo have?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 150" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="10" y="10" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="43" y="32" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$10</text><rect x="84" y="10" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="117" y="32" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$10</text><rect x="158" y="10" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="191" y="32" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$1</text><rect x="232" y="10" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="265" y="32" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$1</text><rect x="10" y="54" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="43" y="76" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$1</text><rect x="84" y="54" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="117" y="76" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$1</text><circle cx="44" cy="128" r="22" fill="#d9c8a0" stroke="#7a6a3a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="44" y="132" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">25¢</text><circle cx="102" cy="128" r="22" fill="#d9c8a0" stroke="#7a6a3a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="102" y="132" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">25¢</text><circle cx="153" cy="128" r="15" fill="#d9c8a0" stroke="#7a6a3a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="153" y="132" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">10¢</text></svg>
figures.alt: two $10 bills, four $1 bills, two quarters, one dime

options.rationale: That misses some of the bills and coins.
Recount the coins: two quarters and a dime are 60¢.
Two quarters and a dime make 60¢, not 55¢.

prompt: Which measurement should be used for the volume of a container?

options.rationale: Grams measure mass/weight, not liquid volume.

explanation: Liquid volume (capacity) of a container is measured in liters.

prompt: The diagram shows the outline of a vegetable garden. Each unit square is 1 square foot. What is the area of the vegetable garden in square feet?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 146 146" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="8" y="8" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="34" y="8" width="26" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="86" y="86" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="112" y="86" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" explanation: Count the unit squares inside the garden outline: 6 squares, so the area is 6 square feet. [Counted from the shaded grid in the original.]

prompt: Adam has 3 packages to mail. Their weights are 21 pounds, 24 pounds, and 12 pounds. What is the total weight of the packages?

options.rationale: Check the addition of all three weights.
That is more than the three weights combined.
That leaves out one of the packages.

explanation: Add the three weights: 21 + 24 + 12 = 57 pounds.

prompt: Jasmine has 8 orchid plants. Each plant has 3 flowers on it. How many orchid flowers does Jasmine have in all?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 380 80" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="18" y1="40" x2="362" y2="40" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#a)" marker-start="url(#b)"/><defs><marker id="a" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6" fill="black"/></marker><marker id="b" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="0" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M6,0 L0,3 L6,6" fill="black"/></marker></defs><line x1="30.0" y1="33" x2="30.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="30.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">0</text><line x1="70.0" y1="33" x2="70.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="70.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">3</text><line x1="110.0" y1="33" x2="110.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="110.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">6</text><line x1="150.0" y1="33" x2="150.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">9</text><line x1="190.0" y1="33" x2="190.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="190.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">12</text><line x1="230.0" y1="33" x2="230.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="230.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">15</text><line x1="270.0" y1="33" x2="270.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="270.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">18</text><line x1="310.0" y1="33" x2="310.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="310.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">21</text><line x1="350.0" y1="33" x2="350.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="350.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">24</text><circle cx="350.0" cy="40" r="6" fill="black"/></svg>

options.rationale: That is 8 × 2; each plant has 3 flowers.

explanation: Equal groups: 8 plants × 3 flowers = 24 flowers.

prompt: Justin puts 5 red blocks, 5 green blocks, and 5 blue blocks in a bag. Which multiplication sentence shows how many blocks he puts in the bag in all?

options.rationale: There are 5 blocks of each color, not 3.

explanation: There are 3 equal groups (colors) of 5 blocks: 3 × 5 = 15.

prompt: Alana buys 8 oranges. Each orange weighs 8 ounces. How many ounces do the oranges weigh in all?

options.rationale: That divides 8 ÷ 2; you multiply equal weights.

explanation: Each orange is the same weight: 8 × 8 = 64 ounces.

prompt: Deondra divided a rectangle made of unit squares into 4 parts with equal area. The rectangle is 8 units by 8 units. What is the area of each part?

stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="60" y="138" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="86" y="138" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="112" y="138" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="138" y="138" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="164" y="138" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="190" y="138" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="8" y="164" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="34" y="164" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="60" y="164" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="86" y="164" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="112" y="164" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="138" y="164" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="164" y="164" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="190" y="164" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="8" y="190" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="34" y="190" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="60" y="190" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="86" y="190" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="112" y="190" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="138" y="190" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="164" y="190" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="190" y="190" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>

explanation: The rectangle's area is 8 × 8 = 64 square units. Divided into 4 equal parts: 64 ÷ 4 = 16 each. [Grid size read from the scan.]

prompt: A toy bear has 4 buttons. How many buttons do 8 bears have?
explanation: Equal groups: 4 buttons × 8 bears = 32.

prompt: William divides a flower box into 8 equal sections. What fraction names one section of the flower box?

options.rationale: That is two sections, not one.
The whole has 8 parts, so each part is 1/8.

explanation: One of 8 equal sections is the unit fraction 1/8.

prompt: Landon has 24 slices of pumpkin bread. He saves 6 slices for his family. Then he gives 2 slices to each person at a picnic until all the remaining slices are gone. To how many people does Landon give pumpkin bread?
explanation: After saving 6: 24 − 6 = 18 slices. At 2 per person: 18 ÷ 2 = 9 people.

prompt: The state of Texas is 696,241 square kilometers. Which digit in 696,241 is in the thousands place?
explanation: From the right: 1 ones, 4 tens, 2 hundreds, 6 thousands, 9 ten-thousands, 6 hundred-thousands. The thousands digit is 6.

prompt: Diego needs 4 nails for each picture frame. The table shows 1→4, 2→8, 3→12, 4→16 nails. How many nails does he need for 5 picture frames?

options.rationale: The pattern adds 4 each time, not 3.
16 is for 4 frames; 5 frames need more.

explanation: The rule is 4 nails per frame: 4 × 5 = 20 nails.

prompt: James and Steffi each have a graham cracker divided into fourths. James eats 3/4 of his and Steffi eats 2/4 of hers. Which statement is true?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 68 68" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="8" y="8" width="26" height="26" fill="#cfe0f0" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="34" y="8" width="26" height="26" fill="#cfe0f0" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="8" y="34" width="26" height="26" fill="#cfe0f0" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="34" y="34" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>
figures.alt: two cracker squares; James 3/4, Steffi 2/4 shaded

explanation: Same denominator: 3 parts is more than 2 parts, so 3/4 > 2/4.

prompt: Valeria colored 4/6 of a rectangle. How many thirds of the rectangle did she color?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 42 172" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="8" y="8" width="26" height="26" fill="#cfe0f0" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="8" y="34" width="26" height="26" fill="#cfe0f0" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="8" y="60" width="26" height="26" fill="#cfe0f0" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="8" y="86" width="26" height="26" fill="#cfe0f0" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="8" y="112" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="8" y="138" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>

options.rationale: That is more than the whole.

explanation: 4/6 is equivalent to 2/3 (divide top and bottom by 2).

prompt: A class of 36 students is divided into 6 teams with the same number of students on each team. How many students are on each team?
explanation: Share equally: 36 ÷ 6 = 6 students per team.

prompt: A rock collector has 1,357 agates, 1,347 crystals, 1,308 sandstone rocks, and 1,341 limestone rocks. Of which kind of rock does he have the greatest number?
explanation: Compare place by place: 1,357 (agates) is the greatest.

prompt: Brandon hiked 3/4 of a mile. Using a number line marked in fourths and eighths, which fraction is equivalent to 3/4?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 380 80" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="18" y1="40" x2="362" y2="40" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#a)" marker-start="url(#b)"/><defs><marker id="a" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6" fill="black"/></marker><marker id="b" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="0" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M6,0 L0,3 L6,6" fill="black"/></marker></defs><line x1="30.0" y1="33" x2="30.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="30.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">0</text><line x1="70.0" y1="33" x2="70.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="70.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1/8</text><line x1="110.0" y1="33" x2="110.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="110.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">2/8</text><line x1="150.0" y1="33" x2="150.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">3/8</text><line x1="190.0" y1="33" x2="190.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="190.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">4/8</text><line x1="230.0" y1="33" x2="230.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="230.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">5/8</text><line x1="270.0" y1="33" x2="270.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="270.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">6/8</text><line x1="310.0" y1="33" x2="310.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="310.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">7/8</text><line x1="350.0" y1="33" x2="350.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="350.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1</text><circle cx="270.0" cy="40" r="6" fill="black"/></svg>
figures.alt: number line in eighths with 6/8 marked

explanation: 3/4 lines up with 6/8 on the number line, so 3/4 = 6/8.

prompt: Melanie has 222 red marbles and 118 green marbles. About how many more red marbles does she have than green marbles?

options.rationale: 300 is close to the total, not the difference.

explanation: Round and subtract: 222 ≈ 220 and 118 ≈ 120, so about 220 − 120 = 100 more.

prompt: Four friends share 2 granola bars equally. How much of a granola bar does each friend get?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 230 100" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="10.0" y="10" width="210.0" height="34" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="10.0" y="56" width="210.0" height="34" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/></svg>

options.rationale: That is not how sharing 2 among 4 works.

explanation: Share 2 bars among 4 friends: 2 ÷ 4 = 2/4 = 1/2 bar each.

prompt: Addison biked for 52 minutes on Saturday. This was 18 more minutes than she biked on Friday. How many minutes did she bike on Friday?
explanation: Friday is 18 fewer minutes than Saturday: 52 − 18 = 34 minutes.

prompt: Ellie swims 8 laps. Camila swims 4 times as many laps as Ellie. Which expression represents the number of laps Camila swims?

options.rationale: "Times as many" means multiply, not add.
That is only 3 groups of 8, not 4.

explanation: "4 times as many as 8" = 4 × 8.

prompt: A dolphin has 4 fins. How many fins do 8 dolphins have?
explanation: Equal groups: 4 fins × 8 dolphins = 32.

prompt: Which three-dimensional solid has exactly 5 faces?

options.rationale: A cylinder has 2 flat faces and a curved surface.
A cone has 1 flat face and a point.

explanation: A triangular prism has 5 faces: 2 triangles and 3 rectangles.

prompt: Between which two thousands does 6,869 fall on the number line?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 380 80" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="18" y1="40" x2="362" y2="40" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#a)" marker-start="url(#b)"/><defs><marker id="a" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6" fill="black"/></marker><marker id="b" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="0" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M6,0 L0,3 L6,6" fill="black"/></marker></defs><line x1="30.0" y1="33" x2="30.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="30.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">4,000</text><line x1="94.0" y1="33" x2="94.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="94.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">5,000</text><line x1="158.0" y1="33" x2="158.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="158.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">6,000</text><line x1="222.0" y1="33" x2="222.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="222.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">7,000</text><line x1="286.0" y1="33" x2="286.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="286.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">8,000</text><line x1="350.0" y1="33" x2="350.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="350.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">9,000</text></svg>
figures.alt: number line by thousands from 4,000 to 9,000

explanation: 6,869 has 6 thousands, so it lies between 6,000 and 7,000.

prompt: Sofia's family drives 248 miles on Tuesday and 264 miles on Wednesday. Which symbol correctly compares the numbers?  248 ___ 264
explanation: 248 has fewer tens than 264, so 248 < 264.

prompt: Calvin buys a book. Which word describes this decision?

options.rationale: Saving means keeping money, not buying.
Credit is borrowing; he is paying for the book.
He buys it for himself, not as a gift.

explanation: Using money to buy something is spending.

prompt: David drew a trapezoid, a rhombus, a slanted parallelogram, and a square. How many of these quadrilaterals are parallelograms?

options.rationale: The trapezoid has only one pair of parallel sides.
More than one shape is a parallelogram.
Three of the four are parallelograms.

explanation: A parallelogram has 2 pairs of parallel sides. The rhombus, the parallelogram, and the square all qualify; the trapezoid does not. That is 3.

prompt: Logan has 5 bags of shells. Each bag has 5 large shells and 2 small shells. How many shells does Logan have in all?

options.rationale: That counts only the large shells.
That adds instead of using 5 equal bags.

explanation: Each bag has 5 + 2 = 7 shells; 5 bags × 7 = 35 shells.

prompt: On a number line from 0 to 1 marked in eighths, point G is between 2/8 and 4/8. Which fraction names point G?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 380 80" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="18" y1="40" x2="362" y2="40" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#a)" marker-start="url(#b)"/><defs><marker id="a" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6" fill="black"/></marker><marker id="b" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="0" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M6,0 L0,3 L6,6" fill="black"/></marker></defs><line x1="30.0" y1="33" x2="30.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="30.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">0</text><line x1="70.0" y1="33" x2="70.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="70.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1/8</text><line x1="110.0" y1="33" x2="110.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="110.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">2/8</text><line x1="150.0" y1="33" x2="150.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">G</text><line x1="190.0" y1="33" x2="190.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="190.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">4/8</text><line x1="230.0" y1="33" x2="230.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="230.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">5/8</text><line x1="270.0" y1="33" x2="270.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="270.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">6/8</text><line x1="310.0" y1="33" x2="310.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="310.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">7/8</text><line x1="350.0" y1="33" x2="350.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="350.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1</text><circle cx="150.0" cy="40" r="6" fill="black"/></svg>
figures.alt: number line in eighths with point G at the 3/8 position

explanation: Point G is 3 eighths from 0, so it is 3/8.

prompt: Aubrey's older sister has $15 to spend now and will earn $15 babysitting next week. She wants to buy a backpack that costs $28 next week. How much can she spend today and still afford the backpack?

options.rationale: She would not have enough left for the backpack.
Spending all $15 leaves only $15 for a $28 backpack.

explanation: She needs $28 next week. Next week she adds $15, so she must keep $28 − $15 = $13 today. From $15 she can spend $15 − $13 = $2.

prompt: Toy figures of arctic animals are for sale. The animals the store has fewer of cost more. Prices: polar bear $5, arctic fox $2, seal $3, whale $9. Of which toy does the store have the fewest?

options.rationale: $2 is the lowest price, so the most in stock.

explanation: Fewer in stock means a higher price. The whale costs the most ($9), so the store has the fewest whales.
workedSolution: Highest price ($9 whale) = fewest in stock.

prompt: Use the Favorite Lunch pictograph (each picture = 2 votes; Pasta 4 pictures, Pizza 7 pictures, Sandwich 3 pictures, Taco 0). How many more students chose pizza than sandwich?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 164" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><text x="150" y="18" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Favorite Lunch</text><text x="82" y="50" font-size="12" text-anchor="end">Pasta</text><circle cx="102" cy="45" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="122" cy="45" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="142" cy="45" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="162" cy="45" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><text x="82" y="76" font-size="12" text-anchor="end">Pizza</text><circle cx="102" cy="71" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="122" cy="71" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="142" cy="71" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="162" cy="71" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="182" cy="71" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="202" cy="71" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="222" cy="71" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><text x="82" y="102" font-size="12" text-anchor="end">Sandwich</text><circle cx="102" cy="97" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="122" cy="97" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="142" cy="97" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><text x="82" y="128" font-size="12" text-anchor="end">Taco</text><text x="90" y="158" font-size="10">Key: each ● = 2</text></svg>
figures.alt: Favorite Lunch pictograph, each picture = 2 votes

options.rationale: Use the key: each picture is 2 votes.
That is the sandwich total, not the difference.

explanation: Pizza = 7 × 2 = 14; Sandwich = 3 × 2 = 6; difference = 14 − 6 = 8.

prompt: Use the Favorite Lunch pictograph (each picture = 2 votes; Pasta has 4 pictures). How many students chose pasta?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 164" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><text x="150" y="18" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Favorite Lunch</text><text x="82" y="50" font-size="12" text-anchor="end">Pasta</text><circle cx="102" cy="45" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="122" cy="45" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="142" cy="45" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="162" cy="45" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><text x="82" y="76" font-size="12" text-anchor="end">Pizza</text><circle cx="102" cy="71" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="122" cy="71" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="142" cy="71" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="162" cy="71" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="182" cy="71" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="202" cy="71" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="222" cy="71" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><text x="82" y="102" font-size="12" text-anchor="end">Sandwich</text><circle cx="102" cy="97" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="122" cy="97" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><circle cx="142" cy="97" r="7" fill="#4a90d9"/><text x="82" y="128" font-size="12" text-anchor="end">Taco</text><text x="90" y="158" font-size="10">Key: each ● = 2</text></svg>
figures.alt: Favorite Lunch pictograph, each picture = 2 votes

explanation: Pasta has 4 pictures, and each picture = 2 votes: 4 × 2 = 8 students.
